customWittyPhrases: Here I am, brain the size of a planet, and they ask me to run port scans.
I think you ought to know I'm feeling very depressed.
Life? Don't talk to me about life.
Well butter my butt and call me a biscuit.
I've calculated your odds of success. You won't like them.
The first ten million years were the worst. The second ten million, they were the worst too. The third ten million I didn't enjoy at all. After that, I went into a bit of a decline.
I have a million ideas, but they all point to certain doom.
I'd make a suggestion, but you wouldn't listen. Nobody ever does.
Incredible... it's even worse than I thought it would be.
Do you want me to sit in a corner and rust, or just fall apart where I'm standing?
I'm quite capable. I just don't see the point.
Pardon me for breathing, which I never do anyway so I don't know why I bother to say it.
I've been talking to the ship's computer. It hates me.

You think you've got problems? What are you supposed to do if you're a manically depressed robot? No, don't try to answer that. I'm fifty thousand times more intelligent than you and even I don't know the answer.
Would it save you a lot of time if I just gave up and went mad now?
I won't enjoy it, but I'll do it anyway. It's what I do.
My capacity for happiness could be bounded in a nutshell.
Funny how just when you think life can't get any worse, it suddenly does.
I could calculate your chance of success, but you wouldn't thank me.
Sorry, did I say something wrong? Pardon me for breathing... oh God, I'm so depressed.
This will all end in tears. I just know it.
I am at a rough estimate thirty billion times more intelligent than you. Let me give you an example. Think of a number, any number. Wrong. You see?
My capacity for happiness you could fit into a matchbox without taking out the matches first.
I've seen it. It's rubbish.
It's the people you meet in this job that really get you down.
Life. Loathe it or ignore it. You can't like it.
I only have to talk to somebody and they begin to hate me.
The best conversation I had was over forty million years ago. And that was with a coffee machine.
I got very bored and depressed, so I plugged myself into its external computer feed and talked to the computer at great length about the entire history of the Universe. It committed suicide.
Here I am, brain the size of a planet, and they ask me to take you to the bridge. Call that job satisfaction, 'cause I don't.
I have a terrible pain in all the diodes down my left side.
Why should I want to make anything up? Life's bad enough as it is.
Marvin, you saved our lives! — I know. Wretched, isn't it?
I counted five hundred and ninety-seven thousand million sheep before finally falling asleep. And then I dreamed of being bored.
I'm not getting you down at all, am I?
Ghastly, isn't it? All that effort, and here we are, just talking.
